title: Uncertainty-aware ensemble with stochastic tree member plus isotonic recalibration
summary: Your current ensemble is mostly deterministic tree blending.
rationale: Your current ensemble is mostly deterministic tree blending. CatBoost exposes `langevin` and `posterior_sampling` for uncertainty-style training, and scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can correct monotonic distortion when enough data is available. A stochastic member can be most useful exactly on ambiguous pit-window rows where binary and rank models disagree.
implementation_hint: Train one CPU CatBoost variant with `posterior_sampling` or `langevin`, or an XGBoost DART model, collect OOF predictions, and calibrate each base learner with OOF isotonic regression before blending. Also try disagreement features such as max-min across members and allow a simple second-stage blender on OOF only.
expected_effect: More diverse errors and cleaner probability shape, which can improve both standalone AUC and ensemble rank ordering on edge cases.
risk: Calibration will not help if base scores are already near-monotone optimal for AUC, and stochastic models are slower/noisier. CatBoost uncertainty options are CPU-only.
